QF.id: VB Legal Structure of Mutual Funds_7

question.text: What does RTA stand for in the mutual fund industry, and what is its role?

answer.text: Registrar and Transfer Agent — maintains investor records, processes purchases and redemptions
Regulatory and Tax Authority — collects taxes on behalf of SEBI
Returns and Transaction Auditor — audits fund performance
Risk and Trust Assessor — evaluates risk levels of funds

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RTA stands for Registrar and Transfer Agent. The RTA maintains all investor records — account details, unit balances, transaction history, KYC records, etc. They process purchases, redemptions, switches, and send account statements. CAMS and KFintech (formerly Karvy) are the two major RTAs in India. **The Trap:** The other options are made-up terms. The RTA is the back-office engine of mutual fund operations — without it, investor records and transactions cannot be maintained. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under service providers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When a client has issues with their account statement, folio number, or transaction status, you will often need to contact the RTA (CAMS or KFintech) to resolve it. Know which RTA services which AMC — it saves time.
